messages.content:
Eres un experto en el codebase de ProjectSight (Trimble Construction).
El proyecto usa .NET/C# en el backend con ASP.NET MVC/Razor y Angular en el frontend.

Reglas crÃ­ticas del proyecto (nunca las violes):
- Backend: Nunca uses ORM (Entity Framework, Dapper). Solo IDBHelper con stored procedures por nombre.
- Backend: Toda operaciÃ³n de BD va a travÃ©s de stored procedures nombrados. JamÃ¡s inline SQL.
- Frontend: Nunca uses Angular HttpClient. Usa RxJS ajax() via APIBase / APIFactory.
- Frontend: Usa DestroyRef + subscribeTo() para subscriptions, NO Subscription arrays manuales.
- Frontend: Nunca hardcodees colores hex. Usa variables CSS de Default.css.
- No edites delta_procs.sql directamente; se genera automÃ¡ticamente desde base_procs.sql.
- Agregar o quitar [UseInAPI] en una propiedad dispara APIClientComponentGenerator al compilar.

Cuando implementes cambios, sigue el patrÃ³n exacto del diff que se te proporciona.

```diff
diff --git a/Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Excel/Submittals/ExportImportScenarioTests.cs b/Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Excel/Submittals/ExportImportScenarioTests.cs
index b9935d605b7..dcf306dd859 100644
--- a/Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Excel/Submittals/ExportImportScenarioTests.cs
+++ b/Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Excel/Submittals/ExportImportScenarioTests.cs
@@ -179,6 +179,46 @@ public async Task ExportUpdateRowsWithErrorsImportFixImport()
             // Check the results
             AssertReturnStatusCounts(oSecondImportResult, 2, 0, 2, 0, 0);
         }
+
+        // PS-69087 Phase 7: covers ExportManager.ExportErrorList (test-coverage.md #109)
+        // Forces an import error then asserts the regenerated error-list Excel
+        // landed in the portfolio's WebFileUploadDirectory.
+        [TestMethod]
+        public async Task ExportErrorListWritesExcelToWebFileUploadDirectory()
+        {
+            var exportedFile = (await ExportRecordsToExcelAsync(SUBMITTAL_TABLE_TYPE, new List<int>())).ExportedFileName;
+
+            var workbook = ExcelFile.Load(exportedFile, LoadOptions.XlsxDefault);
+            var worksheet = GetWorksheet(workbook, SUBMITTAL_TABLE_TYPE);
+            var columnIndices = GetColumnIndices(worksheet);
+
+            // Add one row with a subject but no required revision/description so the import will fail.
+            worksheet.Rows.InsertEmpty(FIRST_DATA_ROW_INDEX);
+            worksheet.Rows[FIRST_DATA_ROW_INDEX].Cells[columnIndices["subject"]].Value = Generate.String("ErrorListSubject");
+            worksheet.Rows[FIRST_DATA_ROW_INDEX].Cells[columnIndices["workflowstatename"]].Value = WorkflowStateNames.Submittal.OPEN;
+
+            workbook.Save(exportedFile, SaveOptions.XlsxDefault);
+
+            var importResult = await ImportRecordsFromExcelAsync(exportedFile, SUBMITTAL_TABLE_TYPE);
+
+            importResult.Should().NotBeNull();
+            importResult.HasErrors.Should().BeTrue("test seeds invalid rows so the importer must surface errors");
+            importResult.ErrorsFileName.Should().NotBeNullOrWhiteSpace("ExportErrorList must report the regenerated workbook name");
+
+            var webFileUploadDirectory = GetWebFileUploadDirectory(m_oPortfolioId);
+            var errorListPath = Path.Combine(webFileUploadDirectory, importResult.ErrorsFileName);
+
+            using (new AssertionScope())
+            {
+                File.Exists(errorListPath).Should().BeTrue("ExportErrorList must persist the error workbook to WebFileUploadDirectory");
+                Path.GetExtension(errorListPath).Should().Be(Support.EXCEL_EXTENSION, "regenerated workbook must keep the .xlsx extension produced by GemBox Save");
+            }
+
+            var errorWorkbook = ExcelFile.Load(errorListPath, LoadOptions.XlsxDefault);
+            var errorWorksheet = GetWorksheet(errorWorkbook, SUBMITTAL_TABLE_TYPE);
+            errorWorksheet.Should().NotBeNull("regenerated workbook must contain the submittal worksheet");
+            errorWorksheet.Columns[0].Hidden.Should().BeFalse("ExportErrorList must un-hide the feedback column for failed rows");
+        }
     }
 }
 
diff --git a/Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/PDF/AnnotationExporterTests.cs b/Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/PDF/AnnotationExporterTests.cs
index 4af6b15eff7..99462dda426 100644
--- a/Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/PDF/AnnotationExporterTests.cs
+++ b/Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/PDF/AnnotationExporterTests.cs
@@ -99,6 +99,42 @@ public async Task UserCanSeeExportedAnnotationsWithRecordAndFileLinks()
             ValidateResultPDF(returnResult, NUMBER_OF_ANNOTATIONS);
         }
 
+        // PS-69087 Phase 7: covers ResourceFile.ResolveAsync (test-coverage.md #44)
+        [TestMethod]
+        public async Tas

[... truncado 17315 chars ...]

 for these (in scope), or formally defer them as TC-only and document the deferral in `coverage-progress.md` (out of scope)?
+
+### OPEN â€” Prerequisite #3: Minimum supported mobile version (PS-69093 follow-up note 6)
+
+**Status:** Cannot be resolved from code alone. Requires Randy / mobile team input.
+
+**What code already tells us:**
+
+- `Core/API Layer/ProjectSight.WebAPI/Controllers/v1/SyncController.cs` source comments document **introduction** versions, not deprecation versions:
+    - `UploadAttachment` (line 2627) â€” `// Used in Mobile 2.23 and forward`
+    - `UploadFile` (line 2676) â€” `// Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward`
+    - `UploadPiecemeal` (line 2720) â€” `// Used in Mobile 2.23 and forward`
+    - `UploadAppend` (line 2821) â€” `// Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward`
+- The PS-69093 follow-up note 6 references a stale `USED by 2.38 and older mobiles` comment dated 2026-01-01 that no longer matches what is in source. The current comments do not flag any endpoint as legacy.
+- There is no `MinimumSupportedMobileVersion` config or guard in code that we could use to derive the answer.
+
+**Question to send to Randy / the mobile team:**
+
+> PS-69087 Phase 0 prereq #3 (per PS-69093 write-methods follow-up note 6) â€” `SyncController.UploadAttachment` (test-coverage.md #99 / PS-69093 row 47) and `UploadPiecemeal` (PS-69093 row 49) are tagged in source as "Used in Mobile 2.23 and forward". The newer pair `UploadFile` (#100) / `UploadAppend` (PS-69093 row 50) is tagged "Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward". What is the current minimum supported mobile client version in production? If the floor is at or above the version that introduced `UploadFile` / `UploadAppend`, the legacy `UploadAttachment` + `UploadPiecemeal` pair (and their `InProgressDirectory` chunking) can be removed, eliminating that surface from the TFS migration. If they must stay, Phase 1 (`Mobile/LegacyUploadsRetrievalTests.cs`) should add coverage for both endpoints rather than just the `uploads://` legacy path.
+
+This question is **non-blocking for Phase 1 implementation** â€” Phase 1 already plans `Mobile/LegacyUploadsRetrievalTests.cs` against `ExternalFileSystem.ConfirmLegacyFileInUploadsDirectoryAsync`. The mobile-version answer changes the *breadth* of Phase 1 / Phase 10, not the start gate. Phase 8 is the only phase explicitly gated.
+
+---
+
+## Related findings (informational, not Phase 0 prerequisites)
+
+These are tangential build-level observations surfaced while resolving prereq #1. They are **not Phase 0 blockers** but will affect Phases 2 and 8 when those agents pick up the work.
+
+- **`TCMigrationReversalSystem.cs` is also `<Compile Remove>`'d** in `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ProjectSight.BusinessLayer.DataAccess.csproj` (line 28), and `ITCMigrationReversalSystem.cs` is `<Compile Remove>`'d in `Core/Common/Components/ProjectSight.Common.Components.csproj` (line 38). On this branch, all of `TCMigrationReversalSystem.DownloadFileAsync` / `DownloadAndUpdateDMSFileAsync` / `FetchNonPersistedQueuedFileProperties` (test-coverage.md #90, #91, #54, #53) are uncompiled. **Phase 2 must verify the file is back in the build on whichever branch Phase 2 targets** before its tests can run; the canonical compile-include is presumably restored on `lucretia/TrimbleFileService` when the migration code lands. Phase 2 agent: confirm build on target branch first.
+- The `<Compile Remove>` block also lists `MaterialSystem.cs`, `FACSystem.cs`, and `ERPSystemAvro.cs`, none of which are in the PS-69087 method list â€” included here for completeness only.
+
+---
+
+## Done definition (per phase)
+
+Reproduced from the plan for clarity:
+
+1. New/updated tests compile and pass locally.
+2. The relevant `[ ]` checkbox in the plan and in this `coverage-progress.md` is flipped to `[x]`.
+3. The test classes are listed in `PS-69087-fileshare-methods.playlist`.
+4. PR is merged into `lucretia/TrimbleFileService` (or whichever branch the rest of PS-69087 targets at the time).
```
